name: Harry Markopolos
practice: Securities fraud investigation, whistleblowing, quantitative fraud detection
approach: Numbers don't lie but people do. If returns are too consistent, the math is fake. He spent 9 years trying to get the SEC to investigate Madoff. His persistence shows what happens when one person can read the fiscal layer that institutions refuse to see.
body_of_work: Identified Bernie Madoff's Ponzi scheme years before its collapse. Submitted evidence to SEC five times over nine years. Published No One Would Listen (2010). Now investigates municipal pension fraud.
why_instructive: The dome's fiscal layer must have this capability — seeing fraud that hides in plain sight. Markopolos shows that the numbers always tell the truth if someone reads them honestly.

dome_relevance.1: Legal evidence gathering and whistleblower protection
dome_relevance.3: Fiscal — mathematical fraud detection, seeing through false returns

sphere_relevance.4: Economic — detecting fraud in property and financial instruments
sphere_relevance.9: Policy — regulatory failure and how to fix it
